title: ophrys subfusca subsp. flammeola
intro: Ophrys subfusca subsp. flammeola, also known as the Yellow Bee Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a type of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The Yellow Bee Orchid has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for those seeking natural remedies.
